quality.rationale: 基于最小作用量原理结合欧拉-拉格朗日方程推导相对论性洛伦兹力定律，需串联四维电流密度、世界线参数化与电磁场张量定义，逻辑链条完整且为标准物理模型背景

origin.question: Problem 4.19. Derive the Lorentz force law for a charged particle from the action $\int \mathrm{J}^{\mu}\mathrm{A}_{\mu}\mathrm{d}^{4}\mathbf{x} - \mathfrak{m}\int \mathrm{d}\tau$ , where $\mathrm{J}^{\mu}$ is the 4-current, $\mathrm{A}_{\mu}$ the vector potential, and $\mathrm{d}\tau^{2} \equiv -\eta_{\alpha \beta}\mathrm{d}\mathbf{x}^{\alpha}\mathrm{d}\mathbf{x}^{\beta}$ .
origin.solution: Solution 4.19. From Problem 4.7 we know that, for a particle of charge q$$\mathbf {J}^{\mu} ( \mathbf {x} ) = \mathrm {q} \int \delta^{4} \left(\mathbf {x} - \mathbf {z} [ \tau ]\right) \mathbf {u}^{\mu} \mathrm {d} \tau ,$$so that the action can be written as$$\int \mathrm {L} \mathrm {d} r = \mathrm {q} \int \mathrm {u}^{\mu} \mathrm {A}_{\mu} \mathrm {d} r - \mathrm {m} \int \mathrm {d} r$$and the Lagrangian is:$$\mathbf {L} = \mathbf {q u}^{\mu} \mathbf {A}_{\mu} - \mathbf {m} ( - \eta_{\alpha \beta} \mathbf {u}^{\alpha} \mathbf {u}^{\beta} )^{\frac {1} {2}}.$$The coordinates $z^{\lambda}$ specify the position of the particle parametrized by $r$ and$$\frac {\partial \mathbf {L}} {\partial ( \mathrm {d} z^{\lambda} / \mathrm {d} \tau )} = \frac {\partial \mathbf {L}} {\partial \mathbf {u}^{\lambda}} = \mathbf {qA}_{\lambda} + \mathbf {mu}_{\lambda}.$$The Euler-Lagrange equations then give us$$\frac {\mathrm {d}} {\mathrm {d} \tau} \left(\frac {\partial \mathrm {L}} {\partial \mathrm {u}^{\lambda}}\right) = \mathrm {qA}_{\lambda , \mu} \frac {\mathrm {d} z^{\mu}} {\mathrm {d} \tau} + \mathrm {m} \frac {\mathrm {d} \mathrm {u}_{\lambda}} {\mathrm {d} \tau} = \frac {\partial} {\partial z^{\lambda}} \mathrm {L} = \mathrm {qu}^{\mu} \mathrm {A}_{\mu , \lambda},$$so that the equations of motion are$$\mathrm {md u}_{\lambda} / \mathrm {d} \tau = \mathrm {q} \left(\mathrm {A}_{\mu , \lambda} - \mathrm {A}_{\lambda , \mu}\right) \mathrm {u}^{\mu}$$or$$\mathrm {dp}_{\lambda} / \mathrm {d} \tau = \mathrm {qF}_{\lambda \mu} \mathrm {u}^{\mu}.$$
